title: French anti-racist activists rally, hoping to uplift fragmented movement
subhead: Many organizations have answered the call to march issued by Bally Bagayoko, mayor of the Paris suburb of Saint-Denis, who faced racist abuse after his election.

title: Fighter jet shot down in Iran, purges within the top ranks: The US military is in turmoil
What is Israel's military objective in Lebanon?
In Japan and South Korea, Macron advocates for a 'third way'
subhead: The French president called for the creation of a 'coalition of independents' committed to a 'third way' set apart from both China and the United States. His counterparts, who value their ties with Washington, responded to his proposal with caution.

title: Pedro Sanchez: Strong on the world stage, vulnerable at home
subhead: The Spanish prime minister commands international stature, notably due to his opposition to the war in Iran. But this recognition abroad hides the fragility of his political base in Madrid.

title: Discovery of 18th-century sailing logbook sheds new light on first French circumnavigation
subhead: Unearthed by a bookseller from the Brittany region, a mysterious logbook is reviving the story of the first French around-the-world sea journeys. Page after page, it narrates a maritime and commercial epic that took place 50 years before the great scientific expeditions.

title: Pre-election Hungary's mood is one of twilight for Orban's rule
subhead: After 16 years of unchallenged rule, Hungary's ultra-conservative prime minister is, for the first time, seeing his supremacy challenged. With days until the April 12 legislative elections, pro-European challenger Peter Magyar is leading in the polls. In the ruling party's eastern strongholds, huge Chinese battery factories have sparked public backlash.

Mysterious 'knocker' continues haunting elderly women in Brittany town
subhead: For the past three years in the village of Goudelin, someone has taken pleasure in harassing women living alone, often elderly, by pounding on their doors in the middle of the night. Around 30 victims have been identified. Speculation is rampant about the knocker's identity, and the victims are finally being heard.

title: Pesticides and cancer: For the first time, researchers find a connection at the national level
subhead: French researchers have developed a model to map pesticide exposure across Peru to reported cancer clusters. They demonstrate a 'robust spatial association' between these chemicals and increased cancer incidence rates in over 400 areas.

title: Mystery among the gorillas: Who killed Dian Fossey?
subhead: More than 40 years after the murder of the primatologist at her camp in Rwanda, the existence of a piece of evidence, possibly kept in a laboratory of the French police, could revive the investigation.
